author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
kabe śrī⋅caitanya more karibena dayā
kabe āmi pāiba vaiṣṇavapada⋅chāyā

kabe āmi chāḍiba e viṣayābhimāna
kabe viṣṇu-jane āmi kariba sammāna

galavastra kṛtāñjali vaiṣṇava⋅nikaṭe
dante tṛṇa kari’ dā̐ḍāiba niṣkapaṭe

kā̐diyā kā̐diyā jānāiba duḥkhagrāma
saṁsāra⋅anala haite māgiba viśrāma

śuniyā āmāra duḥkha vaiṣṇava ṭhākura
āmā’ lāgi’ kṛṣṇe āvedibena pracura

vaiṣṇavera āvedane kṛṣṇa dayāmaya
e hena pāmara⋅prati ha’bena sadaya

vinodera nivedana vaiṣṇava⋅caraṇe
kṛpā kari’ saṅge laha ei akiñcane
en_translation:
When will Śrī Caitanya show mercy to me? When will I obtain the shade of the feet of the devotees?

When will I abandon this false ego of material enjoyment? When will I offer respect to the devotees of the Lord?

With a cloth around my neck and with folded hands, I will approach the devotees. Holding a blade of grass in my teeth, I will stand before them sincerely.

Weeping uncontrollably, I will reveal the multitude of my miseries. From the fire of material existence, I will beg for relief.

Having heard my miseries, the revered devotee will appeal greatly to Kṛṣṇa for me.

By the appeal of that devotee, the merciful Kṛṣṇa will become kind toward such a fallen soul.

This is the humble submission of Bhaktivinoda at the feet of the devotees: "Please be merciful and take this insignificant one along with you."
